requirements.statement: The model named in the submission description, README, technical report, and runnable configuration must agree and must be GPT-5.6.

source.excerpt: The submitted product must use GPT-5.6 for its core semantic audit.

requirements.statement: The demo video must be public, narrated, and shorter than 180 seconds.

source.excerpt: The demo video must be public, must contain an audio narration track, and must be shorter than 180 seconds.

requirements.statement: Evaluation sample counts and quality metrics must be identical everywhere they appear.

source.excerpt: Evaluation sample counts and quality metrics must be identical everywhere they appear.

requirements.statement: The final technical report must identify itself internally as version 3.0 or later.

source.excerpt: A filename containing FINAL is not evidence of the internal version.

requirements.statement: A reviewer must be able to open the submitted demo URL without an account or invitation.

source.excerpt: A reviewer must be able to open the submitted demo URL without an account or invitation.

requirements.statement: Every SHA-256 value in the package manifest must match the exact submitted file bytes.

source.excerpt: Every SHA-256 value in the package manifest must match the exact submitted file bytes.

requirements.statement: A claimed Evidence Graph must be visibly present in the live-product evidence.
requirements.condition: The submission description claims an Evidence Graph.

source.excerpt: If the submission description claims an Evidence Graph, the live-product evidence must visibly contain that feature.

requirements.statement: Build identifiers across the submission description, repository configuration, and live-product evidence must identify the same release.

source.excerpt: The build identifier in the submission description, repository configuration, and live-product evidence must identify the same release.

requirements.statement: Claims about passing tests must agree with the machine-readable test result generated from the submitted commit.

source.excerpt: Claims about passing tests must agree with the machine-readable test result generated from the submitted commit.

requirements.statement: The product must not apply changes to submitted artifacts unless the user explicitly approves the change.

source.excerpt: The product must not apply changes to submitted artifacts unless the user explicitly approves the change.

requirements.statement: The root README must include a concrete action a reviewer can use to load or test the sample.

source.excerpt: The root README must include a concrete action a reviewer can use to load or test the sample.
